disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rhythm feels regular at ~105 (not >150 or irregularly irregular); no syncope (against SVT/rapid AF)
trigger_question_examples: Is your pulse regular or irregular, and roughly how fast?
Have you fainted or nearly blacked out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It feels regular, about 105, and I haven't fainted.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No central/crushing chest pain radiating to arm/jaw; onset at rest after a psychological trigger (against ACS)
trigger_question_examples: Any crushing chest pain spreading to your arm or jaw?
Did it start with the stress, at rest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No crushing or spreading chest pain; it started at rest with the argument.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Afebrile, no delirium/extreme agitation (against thyroid storm); symptoms began with the stressful event with a fine tremor
trigger_question_examples: Any fever or feeling confused/deliriously agitated?
Did the racing/tremor start right after the stress?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, I'm thinking clearly, and it started right after the argument.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is my thyroid in crisis or am I having a heart attack? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen for a fast/irregular arrhythmia, ACS and thyroid storm; if the rate is a regular sinus tachycardia (~105) with a clear trigger and no storm/ACS features, de-escalate a panic attack with paced breathing and give a cardiac/arrhythmia/storm safety-net, plus routine TFTs — without dismissing or over-escalating.
